response: The global food system faces mounting strain as the population is projected to reach 9.7 billion by 2050, requiring 60% more food production. This is made harder by climate change, water scarcity, and soil degradation, while agriculture itself generates about 25% of greenhouse gas emissions and 33% of the world’s arable land has already lost productivity. The text argues that food waste—about one-third of all food produced—and unsustainable farming practices worsen the crisis, with major water sources like the Ogallala Aquifer being depleted. It concludes that scaling solutions such as AI-driven precision agriculture, regenerative farming, alternative proteins, drought-resistant crops, and supportive policy reforms will be essential to keep food affordable and accessible.
